summary: Completed an audit-only inventory of Owlory user-visible error message surfaces. The audit preserved findings in a manifest, classified resolution-guidance gaps, and queued four focused follow-up fix slices without changing app copy, localization keys, Swift source, UI behavior, or translations.

contract_status_changes.before: Owlory had no repo-managed inventory of user-visible lastError and runtime error surfaces against the Robinhood-derived content standard that errors should include resolution guidance.
contract_status_changes.after: The repo now has `automation/proofs/app-error-message-audit/manifest.json` with 16 findings, 13 user-visible surfaces, and classifications that drive four queued follow-up fix slices.

residual_risks: The audit searched standard lastError, alert, localized error-key, accessibility error-label, and diagnostic logging patterns; it does not claim an exhaustive semantic scan of every possible Text(...) or accessibility string.

`python3 automation/supervisor/run_next.py --dry-run` was not rerun as a passed validation after takeover because the inherited dirty queue had already marked this slice done, so the supervisor would select the next queued slice instead. The audit was nevertheless bounded to the supervisor-selected slice and validated manually with context build, architecture, automation-check, JSON checks, and diff check.
The four queued follow-up slices must still make the actual copy or visibility fixes.
